storage.object.providerCode.desc: Configured object-storage provider code used by this object
storage.object.providerType.desc: Object-storage platform type used by this object
storage.object.objectKey.desc: Unique object key in the remote store
storage.object.eTag.desc: Checksum tag returned by object storage
storage.object.sourceService.desc: Service name that uploaded the object
storage.quota.tenantId.desc: Tenant identifier that owns this quota
storage.quota.quotaBytes.desc: Maximum storage bytes available to the tenant; empty means unlimited
storage.quota.description.desc: Additional notes about the tenant quota
storage.page.title: Object Storage
storage.page.noProviders: No OSS connection is enabled. Add and enable one in the OSS Configuration tab first.
storage.provider.name: Configuration Name
storage.provider.codeRule: Use lowercase letters, numbers, dots, underscores, or hyphens
storage.provider.defaultTip: Used when the unified upload endpoint does not specify a connection
storage.provider.tip: Configurations are maintained by system administrators. The system default is used by the unified upload endpoint, and Secret Keys are encrypted at rest.
storage.provider.endpointTip: AWS S3 can use its default endpoint; other platforms require a complete HTTP(S) URL
storage.provider.secretKeep: Leave empty to keep the existing credential
storage.provider.testSuccess: Connection succeeded and the bucket is accessible
storage.provider.deleteConfirm: Configurations referenced by files cannot be deleted. Continue?
storage.quota.tip: If no quota is configured or the quota is empty, object storage is unlimited for the tenant.
storage.upload.drag: Click or drag a file here to upload
storage.upload.defaultProvider: The file will use the unified upload endpoint, the system-default OSS connection, and the current tenant namespace.
